docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
UR5e is a 6-DOF industrial collaborative robot from Universal Robots with a Robotiq 2F-85 adaptive gripper.

  • Robot: UR5e
  • Manufacturer: Universal Robots
  • DOF: 6
  • Gripper: Robotiq 2F-85

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text: Property Value DOF 6 Payload 5 kg Reach 850 mm Gripper Robotiq 2F-85 Repeatability ±0.03 mm

docs.title: Robot Class
docs.text:
File: robots/ur5e/ur5e.py

from robots.ur5e.ur5e import UR5e
robot = UR5e(
    prim_path="/World/UR5e",
    name="ur5e",
    physics_sim_view=physics_view,
)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
robot:
  type: "ur5e"
  position: [-0.5, 0, 0.71]
  press_z_tcp_offset: 0.025

docs.title: Gripper Configuration
docs.text:
GRIPPER_TYPE = "revolute"
GRIPPER_DOF_INDICES = [6]
GRIPPER_OPEN = [0.0]        # radians
GRIPPER_CLOSED = [0.8]      # radians
GRIPPER_MAX_WIDTH = 0.085   # meters

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/ur5e/pick

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: Purpose File Robot class robots/ur5e/ur5e.py RMPFlow controller robots/ur5e/rmpflow_controller.py URDF robots/ur5e/ur5e_robotiq85.urdf Robotiq gripper robots/ur5e/robotiq_2f85_adapter.py Combined USD robots/ur5e/ur5e_with_gripper.usd

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
RoboGenesis provides 10 atomic skills (pick, place, pour, stir, shake, press, pressZ, open, close, move) as building blocks for more complex workflows. Each skill is implemented as a state machine controller with phase-based progression.

All skill controllers follow a unified architecture pattern: self._event controls the current phase, self._t tracks phase progress, and events_dt controls the step increment per frame.

docs.title: Skill Overview
docs.text: Skill Phases Description Controller File pick 7 Pick up object pick_controller.py place 6 Place object at target place_controller.py pour 6 Pour liquid between containers pour_controller.py stir 5 Stir contents in container stir_controller.py shake 10 Shake/mix contents shake_controller.py press 3 Press operation (X-axis) press_controller.py pressZ 3/4 Z-axis aligned press pressZ_controller.py open 8 Open door/container open_controller.py close 3 Close door/container close_controller.py move single Move to target position move_controller.py

docs.title: Skill Controller Architecture
docs.text:
All atomic skill controllers follow the same pattern:

class SkillController(BaseController):
    def __init__(self, name, cspace_controller, events_dt=None, ...):
        self._event = 0      # Current phase
        self._t = 0.0        # Phase progress [0, 1]
        self._events_dt = events_dt or [dt1, dt2, ...]  # Step increment per phase

    def forward(self, ...):
        """Execute action for current phase"""
        if self._event == 0:
            return self._phase_0()
        elif self._event == 1:
            return self._phase_1()
        # ...

        # Phase advancement
        self._t += self._events_dt[self._event]
        if self._t >= 1.0:
            self._event += 1
            self._t = 0

        return action

    def is_done(self):
        """Check if all phases complete"""
        return self._event >= len(self._events_dt)

    def reset(self):
        """Reset to initial state"""
        self._event = 0
        self._t = 0

Phase Advancement Mechanism:

  • events_dt[i] controls step increment per frame for phase i (phase transitions when accumulated reaches 1.0)
  • Smaller value = longer wait = more precise; value = 1 = skip immediately
  • Default events_dt for all skills are centrally managed in controllers/workflow/skill_defaults.py

Gripper Adaptation:

  • GripperAdapter uniformly handles differences between robot grippers (prismatic vs revolute)
  • apply_to_action(joint_positions, gap_meters) - close to specified gap
  • apply_open_to_action(joint_positions) - open gripper

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/pick_controller.py

docs.title: 7 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: Move above object (offset calculated from approach direction)
Phase 1: Horizontal approach buffer (adjust pre_offset_x direction)
Phase 2: Lower to grasp position (add pickz_offset)
Phase 3: Wait for dynamics to settle
Phase 4: Close gripper to grasp (with virtual attach support)
Phase 5: Lift object to target position
Phase 6: Complete

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "pick"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]   # TCP orientation [Roll, Pitch, Yaw] degrees
  events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]  # 7 phases
  pre_offset_x: 0.05                # Pre-grasp X offset (m)
  pre_offset_z: 0.05                # Pre-grasp Z offset (m)
  after_offset_z: 0.3               # Post-grasp lift height (m)
  gripper_distances: null           # Gripper gap (null=from object_properties.yaml)

docs.title: Grasp Offset Calculation
docs.text:
# Priority:
# 1. pickz_offset_override (forward param, for special cases like re-pick from heater)
# 2. config/object_properties.yaml per-object value
# 3. Dynamic fallback = object_size[2] * 2 / 5

docs.title: Virtual Attach
docs.text:
Supports virtual attachment for:

  • Objects declared with needs_virtual_attach in object_properties.yaml (e.g., glass_rod)
  • Robots that force virtual attach (e.g., piper when physical gripper unavailable)

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/place_controller.py

docs.title: 6 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: Pre-place (elevated approach, target_z += pre_place_z)
Phase 1: Lower to target (target_z += place_offset_z)
Phase 2: Wait for stability
Phase 3: Open gripper to release object (via GripperAdapter)
Phase 4: Retreat (X-0.15, Z+0.15)
Phase 5: Complete

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "place"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
  events_dt: [0.005, 0.01, 0.08, 0.05, 0.01, 0.1]  # 6 phases
  pre_place_z: 0.2            # Starting height for descent (m)
  place_offset_z: 0.05        # Hover height above target (m)

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/pour_controller.py

docs.title: 6 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: Move above target (add random height from height_range_1)
Phase 1: Fine-tune position and height (add height_range_2 + object_size/2 + pickz_offset)
Phase 2: Switch wrist to velocity mode, start pouring (positive velocity)
Phase 3: Hold (pause pouring)
Phase 4: Reverse (negative velocity, return to upright)
Phase 5: Hold, complete

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "pour"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
  events_dt: [0.006, 0.002, 0.009, 0.01, 0.009, 0.01]  # 6 phases
  pour_speed: -1                    # Pour angular velocity (rad/s, default -120°/s)
  height_range_1: [0.3, 0.4]       # Phase 0 height range (m)
  height_range_2: [0.1, 0.2]       # Phase 1 height range (m)

docs.title: Wrist Joint Control
docs.text:
• 7-DOF robots: wrist_dof_index = 6
  • 6-DOF robots: wrist_dof_index = 5
  • Phases 2/4 switch to velocity mode, phases 3/5 switch back to position mode

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/stir_controller.py

docs.title: 5 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: Lift/hold current position (Z raised to safe height)
Phase 1: Move horizontally above beaker
Phase 2: Lower into beaker (safe depth)
Phase 3: Stirring motion (circular path, XY offset calculated in real-time)
Phase 4: Lift out of beaker

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "stir"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, -10]    # TCP orientation during stir
  events_dt: [0.004, 0.004, 0.005, 0.001, 0.004]  # 5 phases
  stir_radius: 0.009                  # Stirring circle radius (m)
  stir_speed: 3.0                     # Stirring angular velocity
  target_height: 0.12                 # Beaker height (for safe depth calculation)

docs.title: Safe Depth Calculation
docs.text:
# Ensure glass rod tip stays at least 2cm above beaker bottom
half_h = target_height / 2
rod_extends = 0.12  # Glass rod extension length
safe_min = max(rod_extends - half_h + 0.02, 0.02)

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/shake_controller.py

docs.title: 10 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: Record shake center (first frame gripper_position)
Phase 1: Hold position
Phase 2: Move to center - shake_distance (Y-axis)
Phase 3: Move to center + shake_distance (Y-axis)
Phase 4: Move to center - shake_distance (Y-axis)
Phase 5: Move to center + shake_distance (Y-axis)
Phase 6: Move to center - shake_distance (Y-axis)
Phase 7: Move to center + shake_distance (Y-axis)
Phase 8: Return to center
Phase 9: Complete

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "shake"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 10]
  events_dt: [0.02, 0.018, 0.018, 0.018, 0.018, 0.018, 0.018, 0.018, 0.018, 0.015]  # 10 phases
  shake_distance: 0.1              # Shake amplitude (m)

docs.title: Adaptive Height
docs.text: Shake center is automatically recorded from the first frame gripper_position, adapting to any table height.

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/press_controller.py

docs.title: 3 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: Move in front of target (X -= initial_offset)
Phase 1: Close gripper
Phase 2: Press forward (X += press_distance)

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "press"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 10]
  events_dt: [0.005, 0.1, 0.005]   # 3 phases
  initial_offset: 0.2              # Initial distance in front of target (m)
  press_distance: 0.04             # Press depth (m)

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/pressZ_controller.py

docs.title: 3 or 4 Phases (Optional 4th)
docs.text:
Phase 0: Approach above target (Z += initial_offset)
Phase 1: Close gripper
Phase 2: Z-axis press (Z += press_z_tcp_offset)
Phase 3 (optional): Retract and open gripper (for workflow composition, avoiding semi-closed gripper interfering with next skill)

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "pressZ"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 10]
  events_dt: [0.005, 0.01, 0.01]   # 3 phases (configurable to 4)
  initial_offset: 0.2              # Approach height (m)
  press_z_tcp_offset: 0.031        # TCP fingertip compensation (m, Franka default)

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text: Suitable for press scenarios requiring Z-axis alignment, such as buttons and switches.

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/open_controller.py

docs.title: 8 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: APPROACH - Move to front of handle (X -= approach_offset_x)
Phase 1: FINE_ADJUST - Fine-tune to grip position (X -= fine_adjust_x)
Phase 2: GRIP - Close gripper to grasp handle
Phase 3: ARC_PULL - Arc pull door (rotation interpolation around Z, angle=angle)
Phase 4: IDLE - Wait for door to stabilize
Phase 5: RELEASE - Open gripper to release handle
Phase 6: RETREAT - Move away from door (X -= retreat_x, Y ± retreat_y)
Phase 7: DONE - Complete

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "open"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 110, 0]
  events_dt: [0.0025, 0.005, 0.0075, 0.002, 0.05, 0.05, 0.01, 0.004]  # 8 phases
  angle: 50.0                      # Door opening angle (degrees)
  furniture_type: "door"           # Furniture type
  approach_offset_x: 0.08         # Approach X offset (m)
  fine_adjust_x: 0.015             # Fine adjustment X offset (m)
  grip_offset_x: 0.0              # Grip X offset (m)
  grip_gap: 0.01                   # Gripper closing gap (m)
  pull_time_max: 1.0              # Max arc pull time (Festo uses 12.0)
  retreat_x: 0.06                  # Retreat X offset (m)
  retreat_y: 0.04                  # Retreat Y offset (m)
  retreat_z: 0.0                    # Retreat Z offset (m)

docs.title: Arc Interpolation
docs.text: Uses SLERP (Spherical Linear Interpolation) for position and rotation arc interpolation, ensuring smooth end effector trajectory during rotation around door axis.

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/close_controller.py

docs.title: 3 Phases
docs.text:
Phase 0: APPROACH - Move to front of handle (X -= approach_offset_x, Z += approach_offset_z)
Phase 1: ARC_PUSH - Arc push door (reverse rotation around Z)
Phase 2: RETREAT - Wait for inertia to dissipate, then retreat (retreat_dwell_t)

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text:
skill: "close"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [350, 90, 25]
  events_dt: [0.0025, 0.005, 0.005]  # 3 phases
  angle: 50.0                      # Door closing angle (degrees)
  furniture_type: "door"
  approach_offset_x: 0.05         # Approach X offset (m)
  approach_offset_y: 0.0           # Approach Y offset (m)
  approach_offset_z: 0.1          # Approach Z offset (m)
  retreat_offset_x: 0.2            # Retreat X offset (m)
  retreat_dwell_t: 0.0            # Wait time after push
  retreat_threshold: 0.03         # Retreat distance threshold (m)
  regrip_gap: null                 # Re-grip gap (optional, for openclose composition)

docs.title: regrip_gap Usage
docs.text:
When workflow has open followed by close, the open phase releases the gripper, and close needs to re-grasp the handle. The regrip_gap parameter controls:

  • Phase 0: Open gripper to let handle enter
  • Phase 1: Close to specified gap to grasp

docs.title: Controller
docs.text: controllers/atomic_actions/move_controller.py

docs.title: Single Phase Control
docs.text: Directly moves to target position via cspace_controller, using position threshold for completion detection.

docs.title: Supported Movement Modes
docs.text:
Single-point movement:

skill: "move"
params:
  end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
  position_threshold: 0.02         # Position threshold (m)
  orientation_threshold: 0.1       # Orientation threshold

Multi-segment path movement:

move_controller.forward_multi_segment(
    waypoints=[p1, p2, p3],  # Path point list
    current_joint_positions=...,
    gripper_position=...,
    target_orientation=...
)

Two-segment movement (vertical + horizontal):

move_controller.forward_two_points(
    first_position=p1,       # Intermediate point
    final_position=p2,       # Final target
    ...
)

docs.title: Default Parameter Management
docs.text: All skill default parameters are centrally managed in controllers/workflow/skill_defaults.py:

docs.title: Robot Overrides
docs.text:
Different robots have targeted override parameters:

ROBOT_SKILL_OVERRIDES = {
    "piper": {  # Piper uses [90, 0, 0] orientation
        "pick": {"end_effector_euler": [90, 0, 0], ...},
        "place": {"end_effector_euler": [90, 0, 0]},
        ...
    },
    "fr3": {"pick": {"end_effector_euler": [0, 90, 30]}, ...},
    "ur5e": {...},
    "ur16e": {...},
    "festo": {...},
    "rizon4": {...},
}

docs.title: Parameter Merge Priority
docs.text: YAML explicit params > Robot overrides > Global defaults

docs.title: Skill Registry
docs.text:
Skills are registered in controllers/workflow/skill_registry.py:

from controllers.workflow.skill_registry import create_skill_controller, SUPPORTED_SKILLS

# Create controller
controller = create_skill_controller(
    skill_type="pick",
    name="my_pick",
    rmp_controller=rmp_ctrl,
    robot=robot,
    events_dt=None,        # None=use default
    params={},            # YAML params
    robot_type="franka"
)

# Check support
if "pick" in SUPPORTED_SKILLS:
    ...

  pre_offset_z: 0.05

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Skill Registry maps skill names to controller classes and manages default parameters. It provides the single source of truth for skill-related configuration.

docs.title: Registry Architecture
docs.text:
SkillRegistry
├── SKILL_CONTROLLER_MAP    # Skill name -> Controller class path
├── SKILL_DEFAULT_EE_EULER  # Default end-effector orientations
├── SKILL_DEFAULT_EVENTS_DT # Default phase timings
├── ROBOT_SKILL_OVERRIDES   # Robot-specific parameter overrides
└── SKILL_SUCCESS_CHECKER_MAP # Skill -> Success checker class

docs.title: SKILL_CONTROLLER_MAP

docs.title: Parameter Merging
docs.text:
When executing a skill, parameters are merged from multiple sources:

def get_merged_params(skill_name, robot_type, yaml_params):
    # 1. Start with global defaults
    params = SKILL_DEFAULTS[skill_name].copy()
    # 2. Apply robot-specific overrides
    if robot_type in ROBOT_SKILL_OVERRIDES:
        if skill_name in ROBOT_SKILL_OVERRIDES[robot_type]:
            params.update(ROBOT_SKILL_OVERRIDES[robot_type][skill_name])
    # 3. Apply YAML step params (highest priority)
    params.update(yaml_params)
    return params

Priority (highest to lowest):

  1. YAML step params
  2. Robot overrides
  3. Global defaults

docs.title: create_skill_controller()
docs.text:
Factory function to create skill controllers:

def create_skill_controller(skill_name, task, robot, cfg):
    controller_path = SKILL_CONTROLLER_MAP[skill_name]
    module_path, class_name = controller_path.rsplit(".", 1)
    module = importlib.import_module(module_path)
    controller_class = getattr(module, class_name)
    return controller_class(task, robot, cfg)

docs.title: Adding a New Skill
docs.text:
1. Create controller in controllers/atomic_actions/new_skill_controller.py

  2. Register in SKILL_CONTROLLER_MAP:

"new_skill": "controllers.atomic_actions.new_skill_controller.NewSkillController"
  1. Add defaults:
SKILL_DEFAULT_EE_EULER["new_skill"] = [0, 90, 0]
SKILL_DEFAULT_EVENTS_DT["new_skill"] = [0.1, 0.1, 0.1]

  1. Create success checker in controllers/workflow/success_conditions/

  2. Register checker:

SKILL_SUCCESS_CHECKER_MAP["new_skill"] = "path.to.NewSkillChecker"
  1. Update config in config/atomic_skills/new_skill.yaml

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: Purpose File Skill registry controllers/workflow/skill_registry.py Skill defaults controllers/workflow/skill_defaults.py Success conditions controllers/workflow/success_conditions/ Atomic controllers controllers/atomic_actions/

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Success conditions determine whether a skill has completed its task successfully. Each atomic skill has a corresponding success checker that evaluates the current state against predefined criteria.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
SuccessConditionManager
├── PickChecker
├── PlaceChecker
├── PourChecker
├── StirChecker
├── ShakeChecker
├── PressChecker
├── PressZChecker
├── OpenChecker
└── CloseChecker

docs.title: Base Checker
docs.text:
File: controllers/workflow/success_conditions/base_checker.py

class BaseSuccessChecker:
    def __init__(self, task, controller):
        self.task = task
        self.controller = controller

    def check(self) -> bool:
        """Returns True if skill succeeded"""
        raise NotImplementedError

    def get_failure_reason(self) -> str:
        """Returns human-readable failure reason"""
        return "Unknown"

docs.title: PickChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Object Z position > initial Z + 0.1m

Logic:

class PickChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        current_z = self.task.get_object_position("target_object")[2]
        initial_z = self.controller._initial_z
        return current_z > initial_z + 0.1  # Object lifted

docs.title: PlaceChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Object at target position (within threshold)

Logic:

class PlaceChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        current_pos = self.task.get_object_position("target_object")
        target_pos = self.controller._target_position
        distance = np.linalg.norm(current_pos - target_pos)
        return distance < 0.05  # 5cm threshold

docs.title: PourChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Source beaker tilted AND target liquid level changed

Logic:

class PourChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        # Check source tilted (wrist joint rotated)
        source_tilted = self.controller._wrist_joint > threshold
        # Check target has more liquid
        target_level_changed = (
            self.task.get_object_property("target", "liquid_level") >
            self.controller._initial_target_level
        )
        return source_tilted and target_level_changed

docs.title: StirChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Continuous stirring motion detected

Logic:

class StirChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        # Check stir phase is active
        if self.controller._event != 3:  # Phase 3 is stirring
            return False
        # Check motion is continuous (not stuck)
        position_history = self.controller._position_history
        if len(position_history) < 10:
            return False
        # Compute velocity variance (should be non-zero for stirring)
        velocities = np.diff(position_history, axis=0)
        velocity_variance = np.var(velocities, axis=0)
        return np.sum(velocity_variance) > threshold

docs.title: ShakeChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Oscillation pattern detected

Logic:

class ShakeChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        # Check oscillation frequency
        position_history = self.controller._position_history
        # FFT to detect oscillation
        freq = np.fft.fft(position_history)
        dominant_freq = np.argmax(np.abs(freq[1:])) + 1
        return dominant_freq >= 2  # At least 2 oscillation cycles

docs.title: PressChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Target position reached AND force applied

Logic:

class PressChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        # Check position reached
        current_pos = self.task.get_robot_state()["end_effector"]
        target_pos = self.controller._target_position
        position_ok = np.linalg.norm(current_pos - target_pos) < 0.02
        # Check force applied (via joint torque)
        joint_torques = self.task.get_robot_state()["joint_torques"]
        force_applied = np.max(np.abs(joint_torques)) > force_threshold
        return position_ok and force_applied

docs.title: OpenChecker / CloseChecker
docs.text:
Condition: Door state matches target (open/closed)

Logic:

class OpenChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        door_state = self.task.get_object_state("door")
        return door_state == "open"  # Door fully open

class CloseChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        door_state = self.task.get_object_state("door")
        return door_state == "closed"  # Door fully closed

docs.title: Multi-Frame Success
docs.text:
Some skills require consecutive success frames to confirm completion:

REQUIRED_SUCCESS_STEPS = 5  # Success for 5 consecutive frames

class BaseSuccessChecker:
    def check(self) -> bool:
        current_success = self._evaluate_single_frame()
        if current_success:
            self._consecutive_success += 1
        else:
            self._consecutive_success = 0
        return self._consecutive_success >= REQUIRED_SUCCESS_STEPS

This prevents false positives from transient states.

docs.title: Success Condition Configuration
docs.text:
Success conditions are automatically associated with skill types via the skill registry:

SKILL_SUCCESS_CHECKER_MAP = {
    "pick": "controllers.workflow.success_conditions.pick_checker.PickChecker",
    "place": "controllers.workflow.success_conditions.place_checker.PlaceChecker",
    "pour": "controllers.workflow.success_conditions.pour_checker.PourChecker",
    # ...
}

docs.title: Custom Success Conditions
docs.text:
To add a custom success condition:

  1. Create checker class inheriting from BaseSuccessChecker
  2. Implement check() method
  3. Register in SKILL_SUCCESS_CHECKER_MAP
from controllers.workflow.success_conditions.base_checker import BaseSuccessChecker

class CustomChecker(BaseSuccessChecker):
    def check(self) -> bool:
        # Your logic here
        return custom_condition_met

# Register
SKILL_SUCCESS_CHECKER_MAP["custom_skill"] = "path.to.CustomChecker"

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: Purpose File Base checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/base_checker.py Pick checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/pick_checker.py Place checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/place_checker.py Pour checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/pour_checker.py Stir checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/stir_checker.py Shake checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/shake_checker.py Press checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/press_checker.py Open checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/open_checker.py Close checker controllers/workflow/success_conditions/close_checker.py

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Workflow Engine orchestrates multi-step task execution by coordinating atomic skills. It manages skill transitions, success checking, and held object tracking.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
WorkflowEngine
├── SkillExecutor           # Dispatches skills to controllers
├── TransitionManager       # Handles skill transitions
├── SuccessConditionManager # Per-skill success checking
├── HeldObjectContext      # Tracks held objects
└── StepTracker            # Records execution history

docs.title: Execution Flow
docs.text:
1. Episode warmup (physics settling)
2. Transition settling (if switching skills)
3. Skill execution via SkillExecutor.dispatch()
   └── AtomicController.forward() for each step
4. Success condition checking
5. Transition to next skill or finish

docs.title: SkillExecutor
docs.text:
File: controllers/workflow/skill_executor.py

Dispatches skills to atomic controllers:

class SkillExecutor:
    def dispatch(self, skill_name, target_object, params, held_ctx):
        if skill_name == "pick":
            return self._exec_pick(target_object, params, held_ctx)
        elif skill_name == "place":
            return self._exec_place(target_object, params, held_ctx)
        elif skill_name == "pour":
            return self._exec_pour(target_object, params, held_ctx)
        # ...

docs.title: Parameter Merging
docs.text:
Three-layer merge priority:

  1. YAML step params (highest)
  2. Robot overrides
  3. Global defaults (lowest)
# From skill_defaults.py
SKILL_DEFAULT_EE_EULER = {...}
SKILL_DEFAULT_EVENTS_DT = {...}
ROBOT_SKILL_OVERRIDES = {...}

docs.title: HeldObjectContext
docs.text:
Tracks objects held by the robot:

@dataclass
class HeldObject:
    name: str           # Object name (e.g., "source_beaker")
    usd_path: str       # Object prim path
    attach_frame: str   # Attachment frame
    # ...

class HeldObjectContext:
    def require(self, skill_name):
        """Validate held object for skill"""
        if skill_name in ["place", "pour"]:
            assert self._held_object is not None, "No object held!"

    def set_held(self, obj: HeldObject):
        self._held_object = obj

    def clear_held(self):
        self._held_object = None

Precondition enforcement:

  • place: Requires held object
  • pour: Requires held object (source beaker)
  • pick: Produces held object

docs.title: TransitionManager
docs.text:
Handles clean transitions between skills:

  1. Reset RMPFlow — Clear velocity history
  2. Restore DOF modes — Fix pour velocity mode
  3. Update gripper object position — Sync held object
  4. Reset next skill controller — Prepare for execution
  5. Wait for physics settling — 30 frames default
def transition_to(self, next_skill, scene_objects):
    self._reset_rmpflow()
    self._restore_dof_modes()
    self._update_gripper_object_pose(scene_objects)
    self._reset_next_controller(next_skill)
    self._settle_physics(frames=30)

docs.title: SuccessConditionManager
docs.text:
Per-skill success checkers:

Skill Checker Success Condition pick PickChecker Object Z > initial Z + 0.1m place PlaceChecker Object at target position pour PourChecker Source tilted, target level changed stir StirChecker Continuous stirring motion shake ShakeChecker Oscillation pattern detected

docs.title: Workflow Configuration
docs.text:
workflow:
  table_prim_path: "/World/table"
  language_instruction: "Pick source and pour into target"
  scene_objects:
    - name: "source_beaker"
      path: "/World/beaker_2"
      position_range:
        x: [0.20, 0.28]
        y: [0.05, 0.12]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
  steps:
    - skill: "pick"
      target_object: "source_beaker"
      params:
        end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
        events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]
    - skill: "pour"
      target_object: "target_beaker"
      params:
        events_dt: [0.006, 0.002, 0.009, 0.01, 0.009, 0.01]
        pour_speed: -1

docs.title: StepTracker
docs.text:
Records execution history:

class StepTracker:
    def __init__(self):
        self.history = []  # List of (step_index, skill_name, success)

    def record(self, step_index, skill_name, success):
        self.history.append({
            "step": step_index,
            "skill": skill_name,
            "success": success,
            "timestamp": time.time()
        })

    def get_summary(self):
        total = len(self.history)
        successful = sum(1 for h in self.history if h["success"])
        return {"total": total, "successful": successful, "rate": successful/total}

docs.title: Workflow State
docs.text:
@dataclass
class WorkflowState:
    current_step: int = 0
    current_skill: str = None
    held_object: HeldObject = None
    step_results: List[StepResult] = field(default_factory=list)
    # ...

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: RoboGenesis provides a hierarchical benchmark for scientific embodied agents. This page explains the benchmark design rationale.

docs.title: Hierarchical Structure
docs.text:
Level 1: Atomic Skills (single manipulation)
├── pick, place, pour, stir, shake
├── press, open, close, move
│
Level 2: Skill Compositions (two-object operations)
├── pick + place (object transport)
├── pick + pour (liquid transfer)
├── stir + shake (mixing)
│
Level 3: Workflows (multi-step protocols)
├── heat + stir reaction
├── clean + rinse protocol
├── reagent preparation
│
Level 4: Long-Horizon Protocols (full lab procedures)
└── full_lab_protocol

docs.title: Level 1: Atomic Skills
docs.text:
Single manipulation primitives.

  • pick: Grasp object
  • place: Release object
  • pour: Transfer liquid
  • stir: Mix with rod
  • shake: Agitate container
  • press: Activate device
  • open/close: Manipulate door/container

Evaluation: Per-skill success rate

docs.title: Level 2: Skill Compositions
docs.text:
Two-skill compositions requiring object tracking.

  • pick + place: Object transport
  • pick + pour: Liquid transfer
  • stir + shake: Mixing protocol

Evaluation: End-to-end success

docs.title: Level 3: Workflows
docs.text:
Multi-step protocols (3-5 steps).

  • heat + stir: Chemical reaction
  • clean + rinse: Beaker cleaning
  • reagent prep: Solution preparation

Evaluation: All steps successful

docs.title: Level 4: Long-Horizon
docs.text:
Complete lab procedures (6+ steps).

  • full_lab_protocol: Complete experiment workflow

Evaluation: Full procedure success

docs.title: Success Rate
docs.text: success_rate = successful_episodes / total_episodes

docs.title: Step Success
docs.text: step_success = successful_steps / total_steps

docs.title: Horizon
docs.text: avg_horizon = sum(episode_lengths) / num_episodes

docs.title: Task Complexity
docs.text: Level Complexity 1 Fixed 2 Dynamic 3 Dynamic 4 Dynamic + relations

docs.title: Position Generalization
docs.text:
Objects placed at random positions within specified ranges.

position_range:
  x: [0.20, 0.32]  # Random X in range
  y: [-0.07, 0.03]  # Random Y in range
  z: [0.80, 0.80]   # Fixed Z

docs.title: Material Generalization
docs.text:
Objects with randomized materials for OOD robustness.

materials: ["plastic", "metal", "glass"]

docs.title: Chemistry Lab Tasks
docs.text: Task Description pick Grasp object pick + place Object transport pour Liquid transfer pick + pour Two-container pour heat_stir Heat and stir reaction pick + press + stir Complex workflow full_protocol Complete experiment

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: RoboGenesis uses NVIDIA Isaac Sim with PhysX for high-fidelity physics simulation. This page documents the physics validation methodology.

docs.title: Physics Engine
docs.text: Component Description Simulation NVIDIA Isaac Sim Physics PhysX Scene Format USD Timestep 5ms

docs.title: Default Configuration
docs.text:
physics:
  dt: 0.005              # Simulation timestep
  substeps: 10           # Physics substeps per frame
  gravity: [0, 0, -9.81] # Gravity vector
  solver_type: "TGS"     # Temporal Gauss-Seidel
  contact_offset: 0.01   # Contact distance threshold
  rest_offset: 0.0       # Rest distance

docs.title: Position Accuracy
docs.text:
position_error = np.linalg.norm(simulated_position - expected_position)

Target: 1mm error for static objects

docs.title: Velocity Accuracy
docs.text:
velocity_error = np.linalg.norm(simulated_velocity - expected_velocity)

Target: 5mm/s error for moving objects

docs.title: Contact Forces
docs.text:
contact_force = physics_sim.get_contact_force(body_a, body_b)

Target: Physically plausible force magnitudes

docs.title: Grasp Stability
docs.text:
def test_grasp_stability(gripper, object):
    # Apply grasp
    gripper.close()
    object.apply_random_disturbance()
    # Check object remains grasped
    return object in gripper.get_grasped_objects()

docs.title: Force Closure
docs.text:
def compute_force_closure(object, gripper_contacts):
    # Compute grasp quality metric
    # > 1.0 indicates stable grasp
    return grasp_quality

docs.title: Mass Properties
docs.text:
object:
  mass: 0.5              # kg
  COM: [0, 0, 0.05]     # Center of mass
  inertia: [0.001, 0.001, 0.001]  # Principal moments

docs.title: Friction Model
docs.text:
friction:
  static: 0.8           # Static friction
  dynamic: 0.6          # Dynamic friction
  restitution: 0.2      # Bounce factor

docs.title: Pour Validation
docs.text:
def validate_pour(source, target, expected_volume):
    initial_target = target.get_liquid_level()
    pour_action()
    final_target = target.get_liquid_level()
    transferred = final_target - initial_target
    error = abs(transferred - expected_volume) / expected_volume
    return error < 0.1  # 10% tolerance

docs.title: Stir Validation
docs.text:
def validate_stir(beaker, rod, duration):
    # Track liquid rotation
    angular_velocity = compute_liquid_angular_velocity(beaker)
    # Verify mixing occurred
    return angular_velocity > threshold

docs.title: Drop Test
docs.text:
def drop_test(object, height):
    # Drop from height
    drop_object(object, height)
    # Measure bounce
    bounce_heights = []
    for _ in range(5):
        bounce_heights.append(measure_bounce(object))
    # Verify energy dissipation
    return all(bounce_heights[i] < bounce_heights[i-1] for i in range(1, len(bounce_heights)))

docs.title: Slide Test
docs.text:
def slide_test(object, angle, expected_velocity):
    incline_surface(object, angle)
    release_object(object)
    measured_velocity = measure_sliding_velocity(object)
    return abs(measured_velocity - expected_velocity) / expected_velocity < 0.15

docs.title: Simulation Speed
docs.text:
Target: Real-time or faster (1.0x)

sim_time = physics_sim.get_time()
wall_time = time.time() - start
speed = sim_time / wall_time

docs.title: Physics Stability
docs.text:
def check_stability():
    for body in get_all_bodies():
        velocity = body.get_velocity()
        if np.any(np.abs(velocity) > 100):  # Unrealistic velocity
            return False
    return True

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: Purpose File Simulation Configuration config/simulation.yaml Atomic Action Controller controllers/atomic_actions/ Scene Manager scene_manager/

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: RoboGenesis allows composing custom workflows from atomic skills. This tutorial shows how to create new workflow configurations.

docs.title: Workflow Structure
docs.text:
A workflow consists of:

  • Scene objects: Objects in the scene (beakers, bottles, etc.)
  • Steps: Ordered list of skills to execute

docs.title: Step 1: Define Scene Objects
docs.text:
workflow:
  table_prim_path: "/World/table"
  language_instruction: "Pick source beaker and pour into target"
  scene_objects:
    - name: "source_beaker"
      path: "/World/beaker_2"
      position_range:
        x: [0.20, 0.28]
        y: [0.05, 0.12]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
    - name: "target_beaker"
      path: "/World/target_beaker"
      position_range:
        x: [0.35, 0.45]
        y: [-0.10, 0.05]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]

docs.title: Step 2: Add Skills Steps
docs.text:
steps:
  - skill: "pick"
    target_object: "source_beaker"
    params:
      end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
      events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]
      pre_offset_x: 0.05
      pre_offset_z: 0.05
      after_offset_z: 0.4
  - skill: "pour"
    target_object: "target_beaker"
    params:
      events_dt: [0.006, 0.002, 0.009, 0.01, 0.009, 0.01]
      pour_speed: -1

docs.title: Step 3: Complete Config
docs.text:
File: config/workflows/my_custom_workflow.yaml

name: my_custom_workflow
task_type: "workflow"
controller_type: "workflow"
mode: "collect"
usd_path: "assets/chemistry_lab/lab.usd"

workflow:
  table_prim_path: "/World/table"
  language_instruction: "Custom workflow description"
  scene_objects:
    - name: "source_beaker"
      path: "/World/beaker_2"
      position_range:
        x: [0.20, 0.28]
        y: [0.05, 0.12]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
    - name: "target_beaker"
      path: "/World/target_beaker"
      position_range:
        x: [0.35, 0.45]
        y: [-0.10, 0.05]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
  steps:
    - skill: "pick"
      target_object: "source_beaker"
      params:
        end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
        events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]
    - skill: "pour"
      target_object: "target_beaker"
      params:
        events_dt: [0.006, 0.002, 0.009, 0.01, 0.009, 0.01]
        pour_speed: -1

task:
  max_steps: 2000
  max_episodes: 100
  cameras_names: ["camera_1"]
  cameras:
    - prim_path: "/World/Camera1"
      name: "camera_1"
      translation: [2, 0, 2]
      resolution: [256, 256]
      focal_length: 6
      orientation: [0.61237, 0.35355, 0.35355, 0.61237]
      image_type: "rgb"
robot:
  type: "franka"
  position: [-0.4, -0, 0.71]
collector:
  type: "default"
  compression: null

docs.title: Step 4: Run Workflow
docs.text: python main.py --config-name workflows/my_custom_workflow

docs.title: Simple Pick + Pour
docs.text:
steps:
  - skill: "pick"
    target_object: "source_object"
  - skill: "move"
    target_position: [x, y, z]
  - skill: "place"
    target_object: "target_object"

docs.title: Heat + Stir Reaction
docs.text:
steps:
  - skill: "pick"
    target_object: "beaker"
  - skill: "move"
    target_position: [0.3, 0, 0.8]
  - skill: "press"
    target_object: "hotplate"
    params:
      press_force: 10.0
  - skill: "stir"
    target_object: "beaker"
    params:
      stir_duration: 5.0

docs.title: Double Rinse Protocol
docs.text:
steps:
  - skill: "pick"
    target_object: "beaker"
  - skill: "move"
    target_position: [sink_x, sink_y, sink_z]
  - skill: "shake"
    params:
      amplitude: 0.1
      duration: 2.0
  - skill: "move"
    target_position: [rinse_x, rinse_y, rinse_z]
  - skill: "shake"
    params:
      amplitude: 0.1
      duration: 2.0

docs.title: Pick Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Description end_effector_euler [0, 90, 30] TCP orientation events_dt [0.002, ...] Phase durations pre_offset_x 0.05 Pre-grasp X offset pre_offset_z 0.05 Pre-grasp Z offset after_offset_z 0.4 Post-grasp lift height

docs.title: Place Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Description end_effector_euler [0, 90, 30] TCP orientation events_dt [0.005, ...] Phase durations pre_offset_z 0.3 Approach height

docs.title: Pour Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Description events_dt [0.006, ...] Phase durations pour_speed -1 Pour velocity

docs.title: Stir Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Description events_dt [0.005, ...] Phase durations stir_radius 0.02 Stirring circle radius stir_speed 1.0 Stirring speed

docs.title: 1. Create Controller
docs.text:
File: controllers/atomic_actions/new_skill_controller.py

class NewSkillController:
    def __init__(self, task, robot, cfg):
        self._event = 0
        self._t = 0.0

    def forward(self):
        if self._event == 0:
            return self._phase_0()
        # ...

    def is_done(self):
        return self._event >= self._num_phases

docs.title: 2. Register Skill
docs.text:
File: controllers/workflow/skill_registry.py

SKILL_CONTROLLER_MAP["new_skill"] = "controllers.atomic_actions.new_skill_controller.NewSkillController"

docs.title: 3. Add Defaults
docs.text:
File: controllers/workflow/skill_defaults.py

SKILL_DEFAULT_EE_EULER["new_skill"] = [0, 90, 0]
SKILL_DEFAULT_EVENTS_DT["new_skill"] = [0.1, 0.1]

docs.title: 4. Use in Workflow
docs.text:
steps:
  - skill: "new_skill"
    target_object: "object_name"
    params:
      custom_param: value

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Start simple: Test individual skills before composing
  • Use templates: Start from existing workflows
  • Validate objects: Ensure object paths exist in scene
  • Tune parameters: Adjust timing and offsets per task
  • Test sequentially: Run each step individually first

docs.text: Purpose File Workflow configs config/workflows/ Skill executor controllers/workflow/skill_executor.py Skill defaults controllers/workflow/skill_defaults.py Skill registry controllers/workflow/skill_registry.py

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This tutorial walks through collecting demonstration data using scripted controllers. Data is stored in HDF5 format for policy learning.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• RoboGenesis installed (see Installation)
  • Environment activated (conda activate RoboGenesis)
  • Isaac Sim 5.1 accessible

docs.title: Atomic Skills
docs.text:
Single-skill data collection:

# Franka (default)
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick

# Rizon4
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/rizon4/pick

# UR5e
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/ur5e/pick

# FR3
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/fr3/pick

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text:
Multi-step data collection:

# Pick + Pour
python main.py --config-name workflows/workflow_pick_pour

# Heat + Stir reaction
python main.py --config-name workflows/workflow_heat_stir_reaction

# Clean beaker
python main.py --config-name workflows/workflow_clean_beaker

docs.title: Basic Config
docs.text:
# config/atomic_skills/pick.yaml
mode: "collect"                    # Data collection mode
max_episodes: 100                  # Number of episodes

robot:
  type: "franka"
  position: [-0.4, -0, 0.71]

task:
  max_steps: 1000
  obj_paths:
    - path: "/World/conical_bottle02"
      position_range:
        x: [0.22, 0.32]
        y: [-0.07, 0.03]
        z: [0.80, 0.80]

docs.title: Camera Config

cameras_names: ["camera_1", "camera_2"]
cameras:
  - prim_path: "/World/Camera1"
    name: "camera_1"
    translation: [2, 0, 2]
    resolution: [256, 256]
    focal_length: 6
    orientation: [0.61237, 0.35355, 0.35355, 0.61237]
    image_type: "rgb"
  - prim_path: "/World/Camera2"
    name: "camera_2"
    translation: [0, -2, 1.5]
    resolution: [256, 256]
    focal_length: 6
    orientation: [0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5]
    image_type: "depth"

docs.title: Collector Config

collector:
  type: "default"
  compression: null    # or "gzip"
  chunk_size: 100

docs.title: Step 3: Run Collection
docs.text:
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick

Expected output:

[INFO] Loading scene from assets/chemistry_lab/...
[INFO] Robot initialized: franka
[INFO] DataCollector: Starting episode 1/100
[INFO] Skill execution: pick (phase 0/7)
[INFO] Episode 1 completed, success: True
[INFO] Data saved to: outputs/collect/2026.04.24/12.34.56_atomic_pick/
[INFO] Episode 2/100 completed, success: True
...
[INFO] Collection complete: 95/100 episodes successful (95%)

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
outputs/collect/
└── 2026.04.24/
    └── 12.34.56_atomic_pick/
        ├── dataset/
        │   ├── episode_000.h5
        │   ├── episode_001.h5
        │   └── ...
        ├── success.csv            # Episode success log
        └── config.yaml           # Saved config

docs.title: HDF5 Episode Structure
docs.text:
import h5py

with h5py.File("episode_000.h5", "r") as f:
    # Observation images
    print(f["observation/images/camera_1_rgb"].shape)   # (H, W, 3)
    print(f["observation/images/camera_2_rgb"].shape)   # (H, W, 3)

    # Robot state
    print(f["observation/robot_state/joint_positions"].shape)  # (7,)
    print(f["observation/robot_state/gripper_state"].shape)     # (2,)

    # Action
    print(f["action"].shape)  # (9,) - 7 joints + 2 gripper

    # Metadata
    print(f["timestamp"][()])  # float64
    print(f["success"][()])     # bool

docs.title: Resumable Collection
docs.text:
Use ResumableDataCollector to resume interrupted collection:

collector = DataCollector(cfg)
collector.resume(output_dir)  # Continues from last episode

docs.title: Multi-Robot Collection
docs.text:
For each robot type, create a separate config:

# Collect with Franka
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/franka/pick

# Collect with Rizon4
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/rizon4/pick

Output directories are separated by robot type:

outputs/collect/2026.04.24/
├── 12.34.56_atomic_pick/        # Franka
└── 12.45.00_atomic_pick_rizon4/  # Rizon4

docs.title: Data Conversion
docs.text:
Convert to LeRobot format for training:

python scripts/convert_labsim_data_to_lerobot.py \
    --data_dir outputs/collect/2026.04.24/12.34.56_atomic_pick/dataset \
    --num_processes 8 \
    --fps 60 \
    --repo_name RoboGenesis/level1-pick

Parameters:

Parameter Description --data_dir Path to collected HDF5 episodes --num_processes Parallel conversion workers --fps Frames per second for timestamp --repo_name HuggingFace dataset name

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Collect diverse data: Vary object positions across episodes
  • Check success rate: Target >90% success before training
  • Multiple cameras: Use 2+ cameras for better observation
  • Balance dataset: Collect similar episodes for each skill
  • Validate recordings: Check HDF5 files after collection

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Goal Next Tutorial Train a policy Training Run inference Inference Custom workflow Custom Workflow

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: After training a policy model, you can use it for inference to evaluate task success rate or deploy in closed-loop control.

docs.title: Local Inference
docs.text:
Model runs on local GPU. Best for single-instance evaluation.

Configuration:

infer:
  type: "local"
  policy_model_path: "outputs/train/checkpoints/latest.ckpt"
  policy_config_path: "outputs/train/.hydra/config.yaml"
  normalizer_path: "outputs/train/checkpoints/normalize.ckpt"

docs.title: Remote Inference
docs.text:
Model runs on remote OpenPI server. Best for distributed deployment.

Configuration:

infer:
  type: "remote"
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 8080
  n_obs_steps: 3
  timeout: 30
  max_retries: 3

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Model
docs.text:
Ensure you have trained model files:

outputs/train/
├── checkpoints/
│   ├── latest.ckpt
│   └── normalize.ckpt
└── .hydra/
    └── config.yaml

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Inference
docs.text:
Create or modify config:

# config/atomic_skills/pick_infer.yaml

mode: "infer"
infer:
  type: "local"
  policy_model_path: "outputs/train/2026.04.24/12.43.59_diffusion_pick/checkpoints/latest.ckpt"
  policy_config_path: "outputs/train/2026.04.24/12.43.59_diffusion_pick/.hydra/config.yaml"
  normalizer_path: "outputs/train/2026.04.24/12.43.59_diffusion_pick/checkpoints/normalize.ckpt"

max_episodes: 50

robot:
  type: "franka"
  position: [-0.4, -0, 0.71]

task:
  max_steps: 1000
  obj_paths:
    - path: "/World/conical_bottle02"
      position_range:
        x: [0.22, 0.32]
        y: [-0.07, 0.03]
        z: [0.80, 0.80]

docs.title: Local Inference
docs.text: python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick_infer

docs.title: Remote Inference
docs.text:
# First, start OpenPI server
cd openpi && python server.py --port 8080

# Then run inference
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick_infer infer.type=remote

docs.title: Expected Output
docs.text:
[INFO] Loading model from: outputs/train/2026.04.24/12.43.59_diffusion_pick/checkpoints/latest.ckpt
[INFO] Initializing inference engine: local
[INFO] Starting inference evaluation...
[INFO] Episode 1/50: success=True, steps=156
[INFO] Episode 2/50: success=True, steps=142
[INFO] Episode 3/50: success=False, steps=1000 (max_steps)
...
[INFO] Inference complete: 42/50 successful (84%)
[INFO] Results saved to: outputs/infer/2026.04.24/13.45.00_atomic_pick/

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
outputs/infer/
└── 2026.04.24/
    └── 13.45.00_atomic_pick/
        ├── episode_000.h5    # Inference episode data
        ├── episode_001.h5
        ├── success.csv       # Success log
        └── metrics.json      # Evaluation metrics

docs.title: Inference vs Collect
docs.text: Aspect Inference Collect Action source Policy model Scripted controller Data output Evaluation metrics HDF5 episodes

docs.title: Interleaving Collect and Infer
docs.text:
You can alternate between collect and infer modes:

# Collect more data with current policy
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick mode=collect

# Evaluate current policy
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick mode=infer

# Retrain with expanded dataset
python train.py --config-name=train_diffusion_unet_image_workspace

# Re-evaluate
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick mode=infer

docs.title: Multi-Robot Inference
docs.text:
For different robots:

# Franka
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/franka/pick mode=infer infer.policy_model_path=...

# Rizon4
python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/rizon4/pick mode=infer infer.policy_model_path=...

docs.title: Performance Tips
docs.text:
• Use latest checkpoint: Contains most recent training
  • Batch inference: For multiple episodes
  • Remote inference: For distributed evaluation
  • GPU utilization: Ensure CUDA is properly configured

docs.title: Model not found
docs.text:
# Verify model exists
ls -la outputs/train/2026.04.24/12.43.59_diffusion_pick/checkpoints/

docs.title: Remote server not responding
docs.text:
# Check OpenPI server
curl http://0.0.0.0:8080/health

# Verify port
netstat -tulpn | grep 8080

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: OpenPI (Open Vocabulary Manipulation) provides remote policy inference via WebSocket. RoboGenesis integrates with OpenPI for distributed inference.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────┐       WebSocket         ┌─────────────────┐
│    RoboGenesis  │ ◄─────────────────────► │  OpenPI Server  │
│   (Inference)   │      remote inference   │  (Policy Model) │
└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘

docs.title: 1. Clone OpenPI
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/your-repo/openpi.git
cd openpi

docs.title: 2. Install OpenPI Client
docs.text:
cd packages/openpi-client
pip install -e .

docs.title: 1. Configure Server
docs.text:
File: openpi/config/server.yaml

model:
  checkpoint_path: "path/to/model.ckpt"
  config_path: "path/to/config.yaml"
  normalizer_path: "path/to/normalize.ckpt"

server:
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 8080
  max_connections: 10

docs.title: 2. Start Server
docs.text:
cd openpi
python server.py --config config/server.yaml

Expected output:

[INFO] Loading model from: path/to/model.ckpt
[INFO] OpenPI server started on 0.0.0.0:8080
[INFO] Waiting for connections...

docs.title: Configure Remote Inference
docs.text:
File: config/atomic_skills/pick_remote.yaml

mode: "infer"
infer:
  type: "remote"                  # Use remote inference
  host: "0.0.0.0"               # OpenPI server host
  port: 8080                     # OpenPI server port
  n_obs_steps: 3                 # Observation steps
  timeout: 30                    # Request timeout (seconds)
  max_retries: 3                # Max retry attempts

docs.title: Run Remote Inference
docs.text: python main.py --config-name atomic_skills/pick_remote

docs.title: Request Format
docs.text:
{
  "observation": {
    "images": {
      "camera_1_rgb": "base64_encoded_image",
      "camera_2_rgb": "base64_encoded_image"
    },
    "robot_state": {
      "joint_positions": [0.0, -0.785, ...],
      "gripper_state": [0.04, 0.04]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
{
  "action": [0.0, -0.785, 0.0, -2.356, 0.0, 1.571, 0.785, 0.04, 0.04]
}

RoboGenesis accepts any response containing "action" key:

  • {"action": [...]} - single action
  • {"actions": [...]} - multiple actions

docs.title: Client Implementation
docs.text:
File: packages/openpi-client/src/openpi_client/websocket_client_policy.py

from openpi_client.websocket_client_policy import WebSocketClientPolicy

# Initialize client
client = WebSocketClientPolicy(
    host="0.0.0.0",
    port=8080,
    n_obs_steps=3,
    timeout=30,
    max_retries=3,
)

# Get action from observation
action = client.infer(observation)

docs.title: Connection Failed
docs.text:
try:
    action = client.infer(observation)
except ConnectionError:
    print("Failed to connect to OpenPI server")
    # Fallback to local inference
    action = local_inference(observation)

docs.title: Timeout
docs.text:
try:
    action = client.infer(observation)
except TimeoutError:
    print("Inference timeout, using fallback")
    action = fallback_action

docs.title: Invalid Response
docs.text:
# Client validates response format
valid, action = client.parse_response(raw_response)
if not valid:
    print("Invalid response format")
    action = fallback_action

docs.title: Performance Tuning
docs.text: Parameter Description Default n_obs_steps Observation history length 3 max_retries Retry attempts on failure 3 timeout Request timeout (seconds) 30

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Add authentication to WebSocket server:

# In OpenPI server
async def authenticate(token):
    # Validate token
    return user_id

docs.title: TLS Encryption
docs.text:
Enable TLS for production:

server:
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 8080
  tls:
    enabled: true
    cert_path: "path/to/cert.pem"
    key_path: "path/to/key.pem"

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: Purpose File OpenPI client packages/openpi-client/src/openpi_client/websocket_client_policy.py Remote inference engine controllers/inference_engines/remote_inference_engine.py OpenPI server openpi/server.py

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: RoboGenesis supports training robot policies using collected HDF5 data. Two model architectures are supported: ACT (Action Chunking Transformer) and Diffusion UNet.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Collected dataset in outputs/collect/
  • GPU with CUDA support
  • PyTorch 2.9.0+

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Dataset
docs.text:
Ensure your dataset has episodes:

outputs/collect/2026.04.24/12.34.56_atomic_pick/
└── dataset/
    ├── episode_000.h5
    ├── episode_001.h5
    └── ...

docs.title: Diffusion Model (Recommended)
docs.text: python train.py --config-name=train_diffusion_unet_image_workspace

docs.title: ACT Model
docs.text: python train.py --config-name=train_act_image_workspace

docs.title: Diffusion Config
docs.text:
File: policy/config/train_diffusion_unet_image_workspace.yaml

# Model configuration
policy:
  _target_: policy.policy.diffusion_unet_image_policy.DiffusionUnetImagePolicy
  shape_meta: ${shape_meta}

# Noise scheduler
noise_scheduler:
  num_train_timesteps: 100
  beta_start: 0.0001
  beta_end: 0.02
  beta_schedule: squaredcos_cap_v2

# Observation encoder
obs_encoder:
  _target_: policy.model.vision.multi_image_obs_encoder.MultiImageObsEncoder
  rgb_model:
    _target_: policy.model.vision.model_getter.get_resnet
    name: resnet18
    resize_shape: [256, 256]
    random_crop: False

# Training parameters
training:
  device: "cuda:0"
  seed: 42
  num_epochs: 8000
  lr: 1.0e-4
  batch_size: 64
  gradient_accumulate_every: 1
  checkpoint_every: 30
  val_every: 10

# Optimizer
optimizer:
  _target_: torch.optim.AdamW
  lr: 1.0e-4
  betas: [0.95, 0.999]
  weight_decay: 1.0e-6

# Data loader
dataloader:
  batch_size: 64
  num_workers: 4
  shuffle: True

docs.title: ACT Config
docs.text:
File: policy/config/train_act_image_workspace.yaml

# Model configuration
policy:
  _target_: policy.policy.act_image_policy.ActImagePolicy
  shape_meta: ${shape_meta}
  chunk_size: 100
  hidden_dim: 512
  encoder_dim: 128

# Training parameters
training:
  device: "cuda:0"
  seed: 42
  num_epochs: 8000
  lr: 1.0e-4
  batch_size: 64
  checkpoint_every: 30
  val_every: 10

docs.title: Step 4: Specify Dataset Path
docs.text:
Edit task config to point to your dataset:

File: policy/config/task/dp.yaml

dataset_path: "outputs/collect/2026.04.24/12.34.56_atomic_pick/dataset"

docs.title: Step 5: Run Training
docs.text:
# Diffusion model
python train.py --config-name=train_diffusion_unet_image_workspace

# ACT model
python train.py --config-name=train_act_image_workspace

Expected output:

[INFO] Loading dataset from: outputs/collect/2026.04.24/12.34.56_atomic_pick/dataset
[INFO] Dataset size: 100 episodes
[INFO] Training on device: cuda:0
[INFO] Starting training...
Epoch 0/8000: loss=2.345, val_loss=2.123
Epoch 100/8000: loss=0.876, val_loss=0.789
Epoch 500/8000: loss=0.234, val_loss=0.298
...
[INFO] Checkpoint saved: outputs/train/2026.04.24/12.43.59_diffusion_pick/checkpoints/epoch_8000.ckpt

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
outputs/train/
└── 2026.04.24/
    └── 12.43.59_diffusion_pick/
        ├── checkpoints/
        │   ├── epoch_0000.ckpt
        │   ├── epoch_0030.ckpt
        │   └── latest.ckpt
        ├── normalize.ckpt         # State normalizer
        └── .hydra/
            └── config.yaml        # Saved config

docs.title: Data Quality
docs.text:
• High success rate: >90% episode success preferred
  • Diverse demonstrations: Vary object positions, orientations
  • Sufficient episodes: At least 50-100 episodes per skill

docs.title: Model Selection
docs.text: Model Best For Diffusion High quality, diverse ACT Real-time capable

docs.title: Hyperparameters
docs.text: Parameter Recommended batch_size 32-64 learning_rate 1.0e-4 num_epochs 5000-10000 encoder resnet18 or resnet34

docs.title: TensorBoard
docs.text:
# Start TensorBoard
tensorboard --logdir outputs/train/

# Open browser
# http://localhost:6006

docs.title: Checkpoint Selection
docs.text:
• latest.ckpt: Most recent checkpoint
  • val_loss lowest: Best validation performance
  • mid-training: Balance between loss and generalization

docs.title: GPU Memory
docs.text:
# Reduce batch size if OOM
training:
  batch_size: 32  # or 16

docs.title: Slow Training
docs.text:
# Use smaller encoder
obs_encoder:
  rgb_model:
    name: resnet18  # Instead of resnet50

docs.title: 首页
docs.text:
RoboGenesis · Isaac Sim 5.1 · v0.2 ​已​发布​ ​面向​具身​智能​的​机器人​数据​生成​平台​

​为​科学​具身​智能​体​打造​的​高保真​仿真​与​分层​基准​ —— 7 ​种​机械​臂​、9 ​种​原子​技能​、​可​组合​工作​流​， ​一键​生成​ ACT / Diffusion Policy / OpenPI ​训练​数据​。

​作者​：​待​公布​1,2 1​实验室​单位​ · 2​合作​机构​ ​立即​开始​ GitHub ​源码​ ​论文​ scroll 9 ​原子​技能​ · 7 ​机械​臂​ · 60+ ​组合​任务​ · 10K+ ​演示​轨迹​ · 1 ​论文​ 0 ​机械​臂​平台​ 0 ​原子​技能​ 0 ​组合​任务​ 0 ​演示​轨迹​ ​从​一句​任务​描述​，​到​万条​高质量​演示​轨迹​ —— ​只​需​一行​命令​。 RoboGenesis · ​论文​摘要​ 3 π ​机械​臂​矩阵​：​七款​灵巧​操作​平台​。 Franka Panda Franka FR3 UR5e UR16e Festo Piper Rizon 4 © RoboGenesis Team · ​由​ Material for MkDocs ​构建

docs.title: 面向​具身​智能​研究​的​全栈​基础设施
docs.text:
​生产​级​仿真​、​模块化​技能​、​可​复现​基准​ —— ​开箱​即用​。

  • Isaac Sim 5.1

    ​基于​ PhysX ​的​高保真​仿真​，​配合​照片​级​渲染​、​传感器​建模​与​精确​接触​动力学​。

  • 7 ​种​机械​臂​平台​

    Franka · FR3 · UR5e · UR16e · Festo · Piper · Rizon4 —— ​统一​控制器​接口​。

  • 9 ​种​原子​技能​

    pick · place · pour · shake · stir · press · open · close · move —— ​可​组合​成长​程​工作​流​。

  • ​分层​基准​

    ​技能​ → ​工作​流​ → ​长程​协议​。​在​每个​抽象​层级​上​度量​智能​体​能力​。

  • ​多​模态​观测​

    RGB · ​深度​ · ​点云​ · ​分割​ —— ​多​相机​同步​，​开箱​即​用于​ VLA ​训练​。

  • HDF5 ​数据​流水线​

    ​一键​采集​，​兼容​ ACT、Diffusion Policy、OpenPI ​训练​栈​。

docs.title: 5 ​分钟​上​手
docs.text:
​选择​最​适合​你​角色​的​路径​。

​科研​者​ ​开发者​ ​学生​
  1. ​安装​ Isaac Sim 5.1 ​并​创建​ conda ​环境​ ​安装​指南​
  2. ​跑通​基线​ —— ​使用​ pick_and_place ​采集​ 100 ​条​轨迹​ ​首个​ Episode
  3. ​在​采集​的​ HDF5 ​上​ ​训练​ ACT / Diffusion Policy ​训练​教程​
  4. ​在​工作​流​与​长程​层级​上​ ​对标​基准​ ​分层​基准​
  1. ​克隆​并​配置​环境​ ​环境​配置​
  2. ​接入​你​的​机械​臂​ —— ​注册​ URDF ​与​控制器​ ​新增​机械​臂​
  3. ​从​原子​技能​ ​组合​自定义​工作​流​ ​自定义​工作​流​
  4. ​扩展​ API —— ​注册​新技能​与​成功​条件​ API ​参考​
  1. ​阅读​ ​概览​ ​了解​平台​架构​ ​概览​
  2. ​熟悉​ 9 ​种​原子​技能​ ​与​组合​方式​ ​原子​技能​
  3. ​走​完​一遍​ ​教程​合集​ ​教程​
  4. ​在​单​ GPU ​上​复现​一份​论文​结果​ ​推理​

docs.title: 引用
docs.text:
​如果​ RoboGenesis ​对​你​的​研究​有​帮助​，​欢迎​引用​。

@article{robogenesis2026,
  title   = {RoboGenesis: High-Fidelity Simulation and Hierarchical Benchmark
             for Scientific Embodied Agents},
  author  = {To Be Announced},
  journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:XXXX.XXXXX},
  year    = {2026}
}

docs.title: 概述
docs.text: ​本​节​介绍​控制器​层​ API。​控制器​负责​机器人​动作​计算​和​成功​状态​检查​。

docs.title: BaseController
docs.text:
​文件​: controllers/base_controller.py

​所有​控制器​的​基类​。

from controllers.base_controller import BaseController

class MyController(BaseController):
    pass

docs.title: 继承​关系
docs.text:
BaseController
├── WorkflowController
├── AtomicActionController
│   ├── PickController
│   ├── PlaceController
│   └── ...
└── NavigationController

docs.title: 构造函数
docs.text:
def __init__(
    self,
    task: BaseTask,
    robot: Robot,
    cfg: DictConfig,
    name: str = "controller",
):
​参数​ ​描述​ task ​用于​观测​的​任务​实例​ robot ​机器人​实例​ cfg Hydra ​配置​ name ​控制器​名称

docs.title: forward()
docs.text:
def forward(self) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    ​计算​当前​步骤​的​动作​。
    ​返回​: ​动作​数组​ [DOF + gripper]
    """
    raise NotImplementedError

docs.title: reset()
docs.text:
def reset(self):
    """​重置​控制器​状态​以​开始​新​的​ episode"""
    pass

docs.title: is_done()
docs.text:
def is_done(self) -> bool:
    """​检查​任务​/​技能​是否​完成​"""
    return False

docs.title: close()
docs.text:
def close(self):
    """​清理​资源​"""
    pass

docs.title: 属性
docs.text: 属性​ ​描述​ robot ​机器人​实例​ gripper ​夹​爪​实例​ rmpflow_controller ​运动​规划​器​ skill_name ​技能​名称

docs.title: PickController
docs.text:
​文件​: controllers/atomic_actions/pick_controller.py

from controllers.atomic_actions.pick_controller import PickController

controller = PickController(task, robot, cfg)

​阶段​: 7 ​个​阶段​ (0-6)

​阶段​ ​描述​ 0 ​初始​阶段​ 1 ​移动​到​物体​上方​ 2 ​下降​接近​物体​ 3 ​关闭​夹​爪​抓取​ 4 ​抬起​物体​ 5 ​移动​到​目标​位置​ 6 ​放置​物体

docs.title: PlaceController
docs.text:
​文件​: controllers/atomic_actions/place_controller.py

​阶段​: 6 ​个​阶段​ (0-5)

​阶段​ ​描述​ 0 ​移动​到​放置​位置​上方​ 1 ​下降​接近​目标​位置​ 2 ​打开​夹​爪​释放​物体​ 3 ​等待​稳定​ 4 ​抬起​夹​爪​ 5 ​返回​安全​位置

docs.title: PourController
docs.text:
​文件​: controllers/atomic_actions/pour_controller.py

​阶段​: 6 ​个​阶段​ (0-5)

​阶段​ ​描述​ 0-1 ​倾斜​准备​ 2-3 ​执行​倾倒​ 4-5 ​恢复​直立

docs.title: StirController
docs.text:
​文件​: controllers/atomic_actions/stir_controller.py

​阶段​: 5 ​个​阶段​ (0-4)

​阶段​ ​描述​ 0 ​移动​到​搅拌棒​上方​ 1 ​下降​接触​液体​ 2 ​执行​搅拌​动作​ 3 ​搅拌​完成​ 4 ​抬起​搅拌棒

docs.title: RemoteInferenceEngine
docs.text:
​文件​: controllers/inference_engines/remote_inference_engine.py

​通过​ OpenPI WebSocket ​协议​进行​远程​推理​。

from controllers.inference_engines.remote_inference_engine import RemoteInferenceEngine

engine = RemoteInferenceEngine(
    host="0.0.0.0",
    port=8080,
    n_obs_steps=3,
    timeout=30,
    max_retries=3,
)

docs.title: 构造函数
docs.text:
def __init__(
    self,
    host: str = "0.0.0.0",
    port: int = 8080,
    n_obs_steps: int = 1,
    timeout: int = 30,
    max_retries: int = 3,
):
​参数​ ​类型​ ​描述​ host str OpenPI ​服务器​主机​ port int OpenPI ​服务器端​口​ n_obs_steps int ​观测​历史​长度​ timeout int ​请求​超时​时间​（​秒​） max_retries int ​最大​重试​次数

docs.title: infer()
docs.text:
def infer(self, observation: Dict) -> np.ndarray:
    # ​将​观测​序列化​为​ JSON
    # ​通过​ WebSocket ​发送​
    # ​接收​响应​
    # ​从​响应​中​解析​动作​
    return action

docs.title: 错误处理
docs.text:
try:
    action = engine.infer(observation)
except ConnectionError:
    # ​处理​连接​失败​
    action = fallback_action
except TimeoutError:
    # ​处理​超时​
    action = fallback_action
except ResponseParseError:
    # ​处理​无效​响应​
    action = fallback_action

def _attach_gripper(self, stage):
    """​将​夹​爪​连接​到​机器人​"""
    attach_robotiq_2f85(
        stage,
        robot_prim_path=self.prim_path_str,
        mount_link_path=f"{self.prim_path_str}/flange",
    )

docs.title: 概览
docs.text: ​本​文档​介绍​原子​技能​配置​的​ YAML ​格式​。

docs.title: 配置​结构
docs.text:
# config/atomic_skills/pick.yaml

name: atomic_pick
task_type: "workflow"
controller_type: "workflow"
mode: "collect"
usd_path: "assets/chemistry_lab/pick_task/scene.usd"

task:
  max_steps: 1000
  obj_paths:
    - path: "/World/conical_bottle02"
      position_range:
        x: [0.22, 0.32]
        y: [-0.07, 0.03]
        z: [0.80, 0.80]
      material: "plastic"

max_episodes: 100

cameras_names: ["camera_1", "camera_2"]
cameras:
  - prim_path: "/World/Camera1"
    name: "camera_1"
    translation: [2, 0, 2]
    resolution: [256, 256]
    focal_length: 6
    orientation: [0.61237, 0.35355, 0.35355, 0.61237]
    image_type: "rgb"

robot:
  type: "franka"
  position: [-0.4, -0, 0.71]

collector:
  type: "default"
  compression: null

docs.title: 关键​参数
docs.text: 参数​ ​值​ ​描述​ name atomic_pick ​任务​名称​ task_type workflow ​任务​类型​ controller_type workflow ​控制器​类型​ mode "collect" ​或​ "infer" ​运行​模式​ usd_path assets/.../scene.usd ​场景​资源​路径​ max_steps 1000 ​每​回合​最大​步数​ robot.type franka ​机器人​类型​ robot.position [-0.4, -0, 0.71] ​机器人​基座​位置

docs.title: 倒液​配置
docs.text:
​文件​：config/atomic_skills/pour.yaml

name: atomic_pour
workflow:
  scene_objects:
    - name: "source_beaker"
      path: "/World/beaker_2"
      position_range:
        x: [0.20, 0.28]
        y: [0.05, 0.12]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
    - name: "target_beaker"
      path: "/World/target_beaker"
      position_range:
        x: [0.35, 0.45]
        y: [-0.10, 0.05]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
  steps:
    - skill: "pick"
      target_object: "source_beaker"
      params:
        end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
        events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]
    - skill: "pour"
      target_object: "target_beaker"
      params:
        events_dt: [0.006, 0.002, 0.009, 0.01, 0.009, 0.01]
        pour_speed: -1

docs.title: 概览
docs.text: ​本​文档​介绍​定义​每个​物体​的​几何​形状​和​夹​爪​参数​的​ object_properties.yaml ​配置文件​。

docs.title: 配置文件
docs.text:
​文件​：config/object_properties.yaml

object_name:
  geometry_type: "cylinder"    # cylinder, box, sphere
  dimensions: [0.03, 0.10]     # ​取决于​ geometry_type
  gripper_offset: 0.025        # ​抓取​时​的​偏移量​
  mass: 0.5                    # ​质量​（​千克​）
  friction: 0.8                # ​摩擦系数​

docs.title: 圆柱体
docs.text:
conical_bottle02:
  geometry_type: "cylinder"
  dimensions: [radius, height]
  # ​示例​：radius=0.03, height=0.10

docs.text:
box_object:
  geometry_type: "box"
  dimensions: [width, height, depth]

docs.title: 球体
docs.text:

  geometry_type: "sphere"
  dimensions: [radius]

docs.title: 锥形瓶
docs.text:
conical_bottle02:
  geometry_type: "cylinder"
  dimensions: [0.03, 0.10]  # radius=3cm, height=10cm
  gripper_offset: 0.025      # ​抓取​偏移​ 2.5cm
  mass: 0.5                  # 500g
  friction: 0.8

docs.title: 烧杯
docs.text:
beaker_2:
  geometry_type: "cylinder"
  dimensions: [0.04, 0.10]  # radius=4cm, height=10cm
  gripper_offset: 0.02
  mass: 0.3

docs.title: 移液器
docs.text:

  geometry_type: "cylinder"
  dimensions: [0.01, 0.15]  # radius=1cm, height=15cm

docs.title: 夹​爪​偏移
docs.text:
gripper_offset ​参数​指定​夹​爪​应从​物体​中心​偏移​多远​的​距离​进行​稳定​抓取​。

# ​示例​：​抓取​烧杯​
beaker:
  gripper_offset: 0.02  # ​从​中心​偏移​ 2cm
  # ​这会​影响​拾取​技能​的​前进​方式​

skill: "pick"
target_object: "beaker"
params:
  pre_offset_z: 0.05  # ​额外​的​接近​偏移量​

docs.title: 质量
docs.text:
object:
  mass: 0.5  # ​千克​

docs.title: 摩擦系数
docs.text:
object:
  friction: 0.8  # ​摩擦系数​

docs.title: 关键​文件
docs.text: 文件​ ​描述​ config/object_properties.yaml ​物体​属性​配置​ controllers/atomic_actions/pick_controller.py ​抓取​控制器​ tasks/workflow_task.py ​工作​流​任务

docs.title: 概览
docs.text: ​本​文档​介绍​存储​各​机器人​配置​参数​的​ ROBOT_CONFIGS ​注册表​。

docs.title: ROBOT_CONFIGS
docs.text:
​文件​：controllers/robot_configs/registry.py

ROBOT_CONFIGS = {
    "franka": {...},
    "fr3": {...},
    "ur5e": {...},
    "ur16e": {...},
    "festo": {...},
    "piper": {...},
    "rizon4": {...},
}

docs.title: 参数​参考
docs.text: 参数​ ​类型​ ​描述​ arm_dof int ​机械​臂​关节​数​ gripper_dof_indices List[int] ​夹​爪​关节​索引​ gripper_type str "prismatic"（​平移​）​或​ "revolute"（​旋转​） gripper_max_width float ​夹​爪​最大​开距​ gripper_open_positions List[float] ​夹​爪​张开​时​的​关节​位置​ gripper_closed_positions List[float] ​夹​爪​闭合​时​的​关节​位置​ lula_description_path str ​机器人​描述​ YAML ​文件​路径​ lula_urdf_path str URDF ​文件​路径​ press_z_tcp_offset float ​按压​ Z ​技能​的​工具​中心点​偏移​ default_q List[float] ​默认​关节​角度​ use_mimic_joints bool ​是否​使用​ Robotiq ​夹​爪​的​仿生​关节​ bent_elbow_q List[float] ​弯曲​肘部​姿态

docs.title: 添加​新​机械​臂
docs.text:
​在​ ROBOT_CONFIGS ​中​添加​条目​：

"my_robot": {
    "arm_dof": 6,
    "gripper_type": "revolute",
    # ...
},

​在​ factories/robot_factory.py ​中​注册​：

"my_robot": ("robots.my_robot.my_robot", "MyRobot"),

docs.title: 关键​文件
docs.text: 文件​ ​描述​ controllers/robot_configs/registry.py ​机器人​配置​注册表​ factories/robot_factory.py ​机器人​工厂​ robots/base/generic_arm.py ​通用机械​臂​基类

docs.title: 概览
docs.text: ​本​文档​介绍​工作​流​配置​的​ YAML ​格式​。

docs.title: 基本​结构
docs.text:
# config/workflows/workflow_pick_pour.yaml

name: workflow_pick_pour
task_type: "workflow"
controller_type: "workflow"
mode: "collect"
usd_path: "assets/chemistry_lab/lab.usd"

workflow:
  table_prim_path: "/World/table"
  language_instruction: "Pick source beaker and pour into target"
  scene_objects:
    - name: "source_beaker"
      path: "/World/beaker_2"
      position_range:
        x: [0.20, 0.28]
        y: [0.05, 0.12]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
    - name: "target_beaker"
      path: "/World/target_beaker"
      position_range:
        x: [0.35, 0.45]
        y: [-0.10, 0.05]
        z: [0.06, 0.065]
  steps:
    - skill: "pick"
      target_object: "source_beaker"
      params:
        end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
        events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]
    - skill: "pour"
      target_object: "target_beaker"
      params:
        events_dt: [0.006, 0.002, 0.009, 0.01, 0.009, 0.01]
        pour_speed: -1

task:
  max_steps: 2000
  max_episodes: 100

robot:
  type: "franka"
  position: [-0.4, -0, 0.71]

collector:
  type: "default"

docs.title: 场景​物体
docs.text:
scene_objects:
  - name: "object_name"           # ​唯一​标识符​
    path: "/World/object_prim"    # USD ​路径​
    position_range:               # ​随机​位置​范围​
      x: [0.20, 0.32]
      y: [-0.07, 0.03]
      z: [0.80, 0.80]
    relative_to: "table"          # ​参考​坐标系​（​可​选​）
    parent_object: null           # ​父​物体​（​可​选​）

docs.title: 步骤
docs.text:
steps:
  - skill: "pick"                 # ​技能​名称​
    target_object: "source_beaker" # ​目标​物体​名称​
    params:                        # ​技能​特定​参数​
      end_effector_euler: [0, 90, 30]
      events_dt: [0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.02, 0.08, 0.01, 0.02]

docs.title: 拾取​ + ​倒液

name: workflow_pick_pour

    - name: "source_beaker"
      path: "/World/beaker_2"

    - name: "target_beaker"

name: workflow_clean_beaker

  language_instruction: "Clean the beaker using double rinse protocol"
  steps:

    - skill: "move"

    - skill: "shake"

        duration: 2.0

docs.title: 机器人​特定​工作​流
docs.text:
config/workflows/
├── workflow_pick_pour.yaml          # ​默认​配置​ (Franka)
